summary: The patient has bilateral cornual blockage as shown by HSG; therefore, she should undergo tuboplasty which can open up obstructed parts of the fallopian tube internally. This approach targets the exact issue identified compared to other options that either don’t treat obstruction effectively or are more invasive procedures involving multiple steps instead of direct corrective action at site of pathology. Agent1 reasoned based primarily around identifying an appropriate surgical procedure capable of treating the diagnosed anomaly precisely where needed—cornual obstructions—in order to restore fertility potential through minimally disruptive means when possible.
